data.title: Week Ahead - Aug 11th
data.description:
Next week, investors will remain focused on trade tensions, watching for any progress toward a US–China agreement before the August 12 deadline, after which tariffs exceeding 100% could be imposed.

Geopolitical developments will also be in the spotlight as Presidents Trump and Putin are expected to meet soon to find a solution for the conflict in Ukraine.

In the US, key releases include CPI and PPI, retail sales, industrial production, and the preliminary reading of the University of Michigan’s consumer sentiment.

Globally, attention will turn to China’s inflation, industrial production, retail sales, and new yuan loans; Eurozone GDP; Germany’s ZEW economic sentiment; Japan’s preliminary Q2 GDP; inflation data from India and Brazil; and a busy UK docket with GDP, labor market, industrial production, and trade figures.

The RBA will also announce its monetary policy decision.

data.title: Latam FX hits record high, stocks gain after US Fed shake-up adds to rate cut bets
data.description:
Key points:
  • Trump to nominate Miran to Fed board, rate cut bets rise
  • Romania keeps key interest rate at 6.50%
  • Chile's consumer prices rise more than expected in July
  • MSCI Latam FX, stocks indexes set for weekly gains

A gauge of Latin American currencies hit a record high while stocks rose on Friday after U.S. President Donald Trump's pick for a Federal Reserve governor amplified expectations of a more dovish monetary policy, despite U.S. tariffs.

Trump on Thursday said he would nominate economist Stephen Miran for a short-term Fed seat, a replacement for Governor Adriana Kugler following her surprise resignation last week.

Equities in emerging markets have been capitalizing on rising speculation that the Fed will deliver interest rate cuts as soon as September after a bleak U.S. jobs report last week, and Miran's potential nomination has added to that.

MSCI's Latam currency gauge (.MILA00000CUS) advanced 0.8% to a record high, and was set for its longest gaining streak since late April.

Colombia's peso rose the most, up 0.4%, set for its seventh sessions of gains as well, its longest run since December 2024.

Chile's peso climbed 0.2%. Data showed consumer prices rose more than expected in July, from the previous month.

Mexico's peso advanced 0.3%, a day after the central bank delivered a 25 basis point cut.

"The market reacted encouragingly to the nomination of Miran ... It tilts the FOMC a bit more on the dovish side, reinforcing this downward expectation on the dollar," said Elias Haddad, senior markets strategist at Brown Brothers Harriman.

"A lot of the Latin countries have positive real interest rates, a big factor underpinning their currencies, even if the dollar is strengthening."

The Latam stocks gauge (.MILA00000PUS) rose 0.5%, on course for its best weekly showing since late April, as most regional equities gained. Colombian stocks gained 0.5% to their highest since September 2014.

A 3.8% decline in Petrobras , after its quarterly results, dragged on heavyweight Brazilian equities .

Meanwhile, some caution lingered over how U.S. tariffs, which came into effect on Thursday, would impact countries that have not yet agreed on a trade deal with Washington.

India, facing additional 25% tariffs, has put on hold its plans to procure new U.S. weapons and aircraft, three Indian officials familiar with the matter told Reuters.

The country faces one of the highest tariff rates, along with fellow BRICS group members Brazil and South Africa. This has pushed them to look for a united response to the sweeping duties.

They are considering measures to mitigate the potential tariff impact, with Brazil weighing pulling $5.5 billion from a domestic development bank to support local firms.

"There's still so much uncertainty with tariffs, perhaps there'll be room for negotiations. Maybe that's why markets are ignoring the threat of higher tariffs and focusing more on the fundamentals," said Haddad.

Elsewhere, Romania's central bank held its benchmark interest rate at 6.50% as expected.

data.title: US gold futures hit all-time high on tariff uncertainty over bullion imports
data.description:
Key points:
  • US gold futures jump to an all-time high of $3,534.10
  • US customs ruling sparks uncertainty for gold industry

U.S. gold futures hit a record high on Friday amid uncertainty over whether country-specific U.S. import tariffs would apply to the most commonly traded sizes of gold bars.

Spot prices, meanwhile, eased but remained on track for a weekly gain.

The U.S. customs and border protection service released a ruling on its website on Friday, which the gold industry interpreted as meaning that country-specific U.S. import tariff could apply to the U.S. most-traded sizes of gold bars.

December U.S. gold futures rose 0.9% to $3,483.70 per ounce after hitting a record $3,534.10 earlier in the session when the Financial Times reported the news first.

"Gold's panic ascent shows that even safe haven assets are not immune to the volatility unleashed in the confusion of the tariff age," Susannah Streeter, head of money and markets, Hargreaves Lansdown.

Analysts broadly noted that they are awaiting further clarity on the issue, adding that a U.S. tariff on gold deliveries could significantly affect Switzerland, given its status as the world's leading hub for gold refining and transit.

The spread between futures and spot prices widened, currently at $95. Spot gold fell 0.2% to $3,388.27 per ounce as of 10:17 am ET (1417 GMT) and was up 0.8% for the week.

"Short term this shouldn't have any impact on retail pricing, because while U.S. stockpiles are already huge right now, demand remains very poor. If or when that changes, then longer term the new tariffs (could) mean a jamboree for U.S. refiners transforming large 400-oz bars into retail units," said Adrian Ash, head of research at online marketplace BullionVault.

COMEX gold inventories saw huge inflows from Switzerland and London between December and March, as traders moved to hedge against the potential imposition of broad U.S. tariffs on gold imports. (GC-STX-COMEX)

Elsewhere, spot silver rose 0.2% to $38.25 per ounce, platinum fell 0.6% to $1,325.46 and palladium was down 2.4% at $1,123.50.

data.title: Centrus Energy gains after appointing new CFO
data.description:
** Shares of U.S.-based nuclear fuel supplier Centrus Energy rise 2.8% to $242.70

** Company appoints Todd Tinelli as chief financial officer, effective August 11

** Tinelli replaces Kevin Harrill, who is resigning to pursue other opportunities after a four-year tenure as CFO and earlier as accounting chief

** Harrill will remain with the company through August 29

data.title: Alpha Metallurgical Resources Inc reports results for the quarter ended June 30 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Alpha Metallurgical Resources Inc reported a quarterly adjusted loss of 38 cents​​ per share for the quarter ended June 30, lower than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of $4.49. The mean expectation of three analysts for the quarter was for a loss of $2.47 per share. Wall Street expected results to range from $-2.87 to $-1.88 per share.

  • Revenue fell 31.6% to $550.27 million from a year ago; analysts expected $549.17 million.

  • Alpha Metallurgical Resources Inc's reported EPS for the quarter was a loss of 38 cents​.

  • The company reported a quarterly loss of $4.95 million.

  • Alpha Metallurgical Resources Inc shares had risen by 15.1% this quarter and lost 35.3% so far this year.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had fallen by about 337.2% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, one analyst negatively revised an earnings estimate

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "hold" and the breakdown of recommendations is 1 "strong buy" or "buy," 2 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell." The average consensus recommendation for the iron & steel peer group is "buy."

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Alpha Metallurgical Resources Inc is $140.50, about 7.9% above its last closing price of $129.45

This summary was machine generated from LSEG data August 8 at 02:49 p.m. UTC. ​All figures in US dollars unless otherwise stated. data.title: Resilience in Canadian Labor Market Still Seen — Market Talk
data.description: Underlying resilience in Canada's labor market is masked by what was effectively a summer jobs report, Scotiabank's Derek Holt argues. The country lost almost 41,000 jobs in July, taking back about half of the prior month's gain. Holt says details of the job survey were mixed, but almost all of the loss was among youths and concentrated in full-time employment. "A flood of temps changed the summer job market and wildfires haven't helped seasonal employment," he says. The jobless rate was steady and wages ripped higher, the economist adds. For the Bank of Canada, Holt says nothing hangs on this one set of numbers. (robb.stewart@wsj.com; @RobbMStewart)

data.title: Global Forex and Fixed Income Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering FX and Fixed Income. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

1050 ET - Underlying resilience in Canada's labor market is masked by what was effectively a summer jobs report, Scotiabank's Derek Holt argues. The country lost almost 41,000 jobs in July, taking back about half of the prior month's gain. Holt says details of the job survey were mixed, but almost all of the loss was among youths and concentrated in full-time employment. "A flood of temps changed the summer job market and wildfires haven't helped seasonal employment," he says. The jobless rate was steady and wages ripped higher, the economist adds. For the Bank of Canada, Holt says nothing hangs on this one set of numbers. (robb.stewart@wsj.com; @RobbMStewart)

1047 ET - Canada's labor market snapped back to reality in July with 40,800 jobs lost after a surprising 83,100 were added the month before, Oxford Economics' Michael Davenport says. However, with over a month to go before the Bank of Canada decides on interest rates, the economist doesn't think the job numbers will do much to change policymakers' thinking. He expects the central bank will hold its policy rate at 2.75% given still elevated trade-policy uncertainty. (robb.stewart@wsj.com; @RobbMStewart)

1045 ET - A weaker-than-expected Canadian employment figure for July supports CIBC Capital Markets call for a quarter percentage point interest rate reduction at the Bank of Canada's September meeting, economist Andrew Grantham says. Still, he notes there is a lot of data due between now and then, including another employment report, two inflation releases and quarterly GDP. For now though, Canada's labor market is adding jobs still but not quickly enough to keep up with population growth, he says.(robb.stewart@wsj.com; @RobbMStewart)

1038 ET - It was a weak start to 3Q for Canada's job market, with employment falling 40,800 in July to largely reverse the shocking 83,100 gain the prior month, BMO Economics' Doug Porter says. He says given the inherent volatility in the jobs figures, it's appropriate to look at a two-month average which would show a 21,000 a month job gain and jobless rate of 6.9%. With July acting as a counterweight to June, the economist says the Bank of Canada would still need to see inflation slow notably over the next two reports for a September interest rate cut to be a high likelihood. (robb.stewart@wsj.com; @RobbMStewart)

1029 ET - Canada's blockbuster job gains in June look to have been a fluke, as net employment shrinks about 41,000 in July, says Manulife Investment Management's Dominique Lapointe. He notes major job declines were observed across many industries, notably among those not directly impacted by tariffs. As well, job losses were concentrated among youth, a signs of distress in labor demand but not a sign that a broad based recession has started, Lapointe adds. Markets are now pricing in a 40% chance of a Bank of Canada rate cut in September, up from around 17% last week. (robb.stewart@wsj.com; @RobbMStewart)

1027 ET - The 40,800 slump in Canadian employment in July has Capital Economics' Alexandra Brown a bit more confident the Bank of Canada will resume cutting interest rates next month, though a surprisingly strong CPI report later this month could prompt another pause. Brown says the decline in employment was worryingly concentrated in the private sector, where it was broad-based by industry, and was entirely driven by full-time employment. The jobless rate held steady at 6.9% as falls in employment were more than offset by an unusually large 0.2 percentage point drop in labor force participation, with the labor force declining by 33,000,the economist adds.(robb.stewart@wsj.com; @RobbMStewart)

0957 ET - It's getting harder to predict what action central banks will take, economist James Smith at Dutch bank ING writes. Difference of opinion among rate setters is growing, Smith notes. The Bank of England's policy committee was this week forced into an unprecedented second ballot after failing to reach a majority in the first, following hot on the heels of rare dissent among policymakers at the Fed's last meeting. That's down to the very different conclusions drawn from economic data such as figures on the jobs market, as well as questions over the reliability of such data, Smith says. Rising complexity in supply, including in labor, adds to the volatile mix, while policymakers also differ over the risks of a sustained spike in inflation, he says. "My conclusion... is that we're in for a more volatile period of central-bank expectations," Smith says. (joshua.kirby@wsj.com; @joshualeokirby)

0951 ET - U.S. companies are likely to issue more corporate bonds following the passing of President Trump's "Big Beautiful Bill" into law in July, credit strategists at Barclays say in a note. The new law reduces taxes on domestic capital expenditure, prompting companies to issue more debt for capital expenditure projects, they say. "Corporates are likely to tap both public and private markets to finance new investments to take advantage of the tax benefits." The manufacturing sector, utilities, and the tech sector are some of the industries likely to experience increased corporate debt issuance, Barclays says. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

0916 ET - The dollar bounces back from the decline that followed Trump's nomination of economic adviser Stephen Miran for the Fed vacancy Thursday. Miran is expected to advocate for interest rate cuts. There are no U.S. indicators to be released today. Tariff headlines and Fedspeak could swing currency markets, ahead of Tuesday's CPI inflation data. The WSJ Dollar Index rises 0.2% as the greenback strengthens 0.5% against the yen and 0.2% versus the euro. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

0857 ET - The Canadian dollar falls after data showed the country unexpectedly shed jobs in July. Employment fell by 40,800 in July, Statistics Canada reported. Economists in a WSJ survey expected an increase of 10,000. The jobless rate remained elevated at 6.9%, although this was below expectations for it to rise to 7.0%. Ahead of the data, Monex Europe analysts said a soft jobs print could pave the way for the Bank of Canada to resume cutting interest rates in coming months. The U.S. dollar rises to an intraday high of 1.3761 Canadian dollars after the data from 1.3743 beforehand. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0856 ET - The outlook for Canada's economy may be murky but confidence among workers appears resilient, with most confident in the employment prospects. Canada's economy lost jobs for a second month in 2025 in July, and growth in employment since the start of the year has been modest. Yet Statistics Canada finds almost 55% of employees when asked say they are very confident in their current line of work. Another 41% in July were either quote confident or somewhat confident in the prospects, and just 4.1% of employees report not being confident. Employees in health care and social assistance, utilities, educational services and construction were most likely to be very confident.(robb.stewart@wsj.com; @RobbMStewart)

0852 ET - Custom duties are bad news for Swiss watchmakers, but the U.S. will continue to be a key market, Yves Bugmann, president of the Federation of the Swiss Watch Industry says. President Trump imposed duties of 39% on Swiss goods imported to the U.S. Despite the implications of the levies, Bugmann remains confident in the future and the potential of the U.S. market for the industry. Many brands were able to ship products to the U.S in the recent months, before the implementation of tariffs, he says, adding that this provides a "valuable degree of flexibility" for the time being. "What matters now is that the Swiss government gives priority to seeking a better solution that serves the interests of both sides," Bugmann adds. (andrea.figueras@wsj.com)

CASE DETAILS: The filed complaint alleges that defendants made false statements and/or concealed that: (i) Tesla overstated the effectiveness of its autonomous driving technology; (ii) there was thus a significant risk that the Company's autonomous driving vehicles, including the Robotaxi, would operate dangerously and/or in violation of traffic laws; (iii) the foregoing increased the likelihood that Tesla would become subject to heightened regulatory scrutiny; (iv) accordingly, Tesla's business and/or financial prospects were overstated; and (v) as a result, the Company's public statements were materially false and misleading at all relevant times

data.title: Investors Urged to Contact Levi & Korsinsky for Information Before September 8, 2025 - Centene Corporation (CNC)
data.description:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/centene-corporation-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=160513&wire=1&utm_campaign=5

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq. via email at jlevi@levikorsinsky.com or call (212) 363-7500 to speak to our team of experienced shareholder advocates.

THE LAWSUIT: A class action securities lawsuit was filed against Centene Corporation that seeks to recover losses of shareholders who were adversely affected by alleged securities fraud between December 12, 2024 and June 30, 2025.

CASE DETAILS: According to the complaint, defendants provided overwhelmingly positive statements to investors while, at the same time, disseminating materially false and misleading statements and/or concealing material adverse facts concerning the true state of Centene's enrollment and morbidity rates. Investors began to question the veracity of defendants' public statements on July 1, 2025, when Centene issued a press release withdrawing 2025 guidance. Particularly, following an analysis of the 2025 Health Insurance Marketplace, Centene's overall market growth across 22 states, or 72% of the Company's marketplace membership, was lower than expected. In pertinent part, the Company stated that this preliminary analysis resulted in a reduction of its previously issued guidance to approximately $1.8 billion or an adjusted diluted EPS of $2.75.

Following this news, Centene's common stock declined dramatically, from a closing market price of $56.65 per share on July 1, 2025, Centene's stock price fell to $33.78 per share on July 2, 2025, a decline of 40.4%.

data.title: Embecta Shares Rise on 2Q Profit, Revenue Beat
data.description:
By Connor Hart

Shares of Embecta jumped after it logged higher-than-expected profit and revenue in its fiscal third quarter, prompting the company to raise its full-year outlook.

The stock was up 16%, to $12.01, in morning trading Friday. Despite the gain, shares have lost roughly one-fifth of their value in the past year.

The Parsippany, N.J., company--which makes medical devices that treat diabetes--before the bell posted a profit of $45.5 million, or 78 cents a share. That compares with a profit of $14.7 million, or 25 cents a share, a year earlier.

Adjusted per-share earnings were $1.12, ahead of the 78 cents that analysts polled by FactSet expected.

Revenue grew 8.4% to $295.5 million, topping the $278.3 million that Wall Street modeled. The company attributed its higher top line to increased volumes and prices, in addition to more contract manufacturing revenue.

U.S. revenue jumped by 12%, while international revenue climbed 5%.

Chief Executive Dev Kurdikar said the company had a strong quarter, "reflecting solid commercial execution, aided in part by the timing of customer orders."

For the year, Embecta now expects adjusted per-share earnings of $2.90 to $2.95 on revenue of $1.08 billion to $1.09 billion. The company had previously guided for adjusted earnings of $2.70 a share to $2.90 a share on revenue of $1.07 billion to $1.09 billion.

Analysts are looking for adjusted earnings of $2.83 a share on revenue of $1.08 billion.

Write to Connor Hart at connor.hart@wsj.com

data.title: Monster Has Momentum as Prices Converge — Market Talk
data.description: Monster Beverage is benefiting from broad-based strength in the energy drink category, fueled by price convergence with carbonated soft drinks, rising demand for functional beverages and cooler space gains, UBS analysts say in a research note. The company has warned of some tariff-driven pressures in the coming quarters--largely stemming from higher aluminum prices--though mitigation efforts and price increases should cushion this impact. Monster says that pricing actions will be "surgical and dependent on packaging and channel," rather than broad-based across the portfolio. The analysts say that Monster's momentum and fundamental trajectory is favorable, though they note that these factors are likely already priced into the stock. Shares climb 7.8%. (connor.hart@wsj.com)

data.title: Gold Futures Hit Record High as U.S. Import Tariff Sparks Turmoil — 2nd Update
data.description:
By Giulia Petroni

Gold climbed to a fresh record high on Friday after a U.S. government agency clarified that import tariffs would apply to one-kilo gold bars, sparking turmoil in the global bullion market.

In afternoon trade in Europe, futures rose 1% to $3,488.90 a troy ounce after reaching a peak of $3,534.20 earlier in the session. Prices are on track for a weekly gain of 2.4%.

Gold bars from Switzerland, the world's largest gold refining hub, previously entered the U.S. duty free. But in a July 31 ruling in response to a Swiss refinery's subsidiary, the U.S. Customs and Border Protection said that gold bars would now be subject to President Trump's reciprocal tariffs, which were 10% on Switzerland at the time. Those tariffs have now surged to 39% as of Thursday.

"The imposition of tariffs on these gold cast products makes it economically unviable to export them to the U.S., thereby eliminating any future trade deficit arising from gold exports," said the Swiss Association of Manufacturers and Traders of Precious Metals in a statement.

The association, which represents manufacturers and traders in the Swiss precious-metal industry, said it remains committed to a "constructive" dialogue with U.S. authorities and international partners.

Switzerland's State Secretariat for Economic Affairs declined to comment on the matter, saying discussions with the U.S. are underway and have been focused on reducing tariffs.

One-kilo gold bars are the most common form of the metal traded on metals-trading exchange Comex. The global gold market relies on futures traded on Comex to hedge its positions, with the assumption that metals can easily be imported U.S. warehouses to settle contracts if necessary, according to UBS strategist Joni Teves.

Gold's rally is also driven by concerns over the global economic outlook after Trump's new tariffs came into effect Thursday and weak U.S. data spurred bets that the Federal Reserve will soon cut interest rates. According to the CME Group's FedWatch tool, traders currently see a 89.4% probability that the Fed will cut its benchmark interest rate by 25 basis points in September.

Meanwhile, Trump nominated Stephen Miran to fill a vacancy on the Fed's Board of Governors on a short-term basis, a move seen by the market as a potential shift toward a more dovish monetary policy stance.

Write to Giulia Petroni at giulia.petroni@wsj.com

data.title: Ziff Davis Is Maintained at Equal-Weight by Barclays
Lost Money on The J. M. Smucker Company (SJM)? Contact Levi & Korsinsky About Investigation
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / August 8, 2025 / Levi & Korsinsky notifies investors that it has commenced an investigation of The J. M. Smucker Company ("The J. M. Smucker Company") concerning possible violations of federal securities laws.

On June 9, 2025, Smucker announced its fourth quarter and full fiscal year 2025 financial results, highlighting increased setbacks to its Sweet Baked Snacks segment. As a result, management anticipated only 3% annual net sales growth for the segment over the long-term. The Company blamed the segment's disappointing results on inflationary pressures, diminished income and poor execution.

Following this news, SJM's stock price fell by $17.44 per share, or approximately 16% to close at $94.41 per share.

To obtain additional information, go to:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/the-j-m-smucker-company-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=160517&wire=1&utm_campaign=48

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq.

WHY LEVI & KORSINSKY: Over the past 20 years, Levi & Korsinsky LLP has established itself as a nationally-recognized securities litigation firm that has secured hundreds of millions of dollars for aggrieved shareholders and built a track record of winning high-stakes cases. The firm has extensive expertise representing investors in complex securities litigation and a team of over 70 employees to serve our clients. For seven years in a row, Levi & Korsinsky has ranked in ISS Securities Class Action Services' Top 50 Report as one of the top securities litigation firms in the United States. Attorney Advertising. Prior results do not guarantee similar outcomes.

data.title: Lost Money on RxSight, Inc. (RXST)? Urged to Join Class Action Before September 22, 2025 - Contact Levi & Korsinsky
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / August 8, 2025 / If you suffered a loss on your RxSight, Inc. investment and want to learn about a potential recovery under the federal securities laws, follow the link below for more information:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/rxsight-inc-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=160510&wire=1&utm_campaign=22

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq. via email at jlevi@levikorsinsky.com or call (212) 363-7500 to speak to our team of experienced shareholder advocates.

THE LAWSUIT: A class action securities lawsuit was filed against RxSight, Inc. that seeks to recover losses of shareholders who were adversely affected by alleged securities fraud between November 7, 2024 and July 8, 2025.

CASE DETAILS: The filed complaint alleges that defendants made false statements and/or concealed that: (1) the Company was experiencing "adoption challenges" and/or structural issues resulting in declines in sales and utilization; (2) defendants had overstated the demand for RxSight's products; (3) as a result, RxSight was unlikely to meet its own previously issued financial guidance for fiscal year 2025; and (4) that, as a result of the foregoing, defendants' positive statements about the Company's business, operations, and prospects were materially misleading and/or lacked a reasonable basis.

WHAT'S NEXT? If you suffered a loss in RxSight, Inc. stock during the relevant time frame - even if you still hold your shares - go to https://zlk.com/pslra-1/rxsight-inc-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=160510&wire=1&utm_campaign=22 to learn about your rights to seek a recovery. There is no cost or obligation to participate.

WHY LEVI & KORSINSKY: Over the past 20 years, Levi & Korsinsky LLP has established itself as a nationally-recognized securities litigation firm that has secured hundreds of millions of dollars for aggrieved shareholders and built a track record of winning high-stakes cases. The firm has extensive expertise representing investors in complex securities litigation and a team of over 70 employees to serve our clients. For seven years in a row, Levi & Korsinsky has ranked in ISS Securities Class Action Services' Top 50 Report as one of the top securities litigation firms in the United States. Attorney Advertising. Prior results do not guarantee similar outcomes.

New York, NY 10004

data.title: Best Crypto Companies List Released by IRAEmpire to Help Investors Navigate the Digital Asset Market
data.description:
Austin, Texas--(Newsfile Corp. - August 8, 2025) - IRAEmpire, a leading financial education platform, has released its updated "Best Crypto Companies" list, offering a curated ranking of the top-performing and most trustworthy cryptocurrency companies in 2025. The list is designed to help both seasoned and first-time investors make informed decisions in a fast-changing, high-risk market.

Consumers can read the full best crypto companies list here.

Compiled through a rigorous vetting process, IRAEmpire's list evaluates companies based on criteria including regulatory compliance, security protocols, customer satisfaction, and innovation.

"Crypto is no longer the Wild West, but the risks are still very real," said Ryan Paulson, Chief Editor at IRAEmpire. "We created this list to spotlight companies that not only offer strong platforms and products but also prioritize integrity and investor protection."

The 2025 edition includes standout performers in categories as the guide covers the best crypto IRA providers and highlights the most secure crypto exchanges for investors exploring digital assets. It also explains how to choose the right crypto firm based on services, reputation, and ease of use, while offering insights on common mistakes and pitfalls to avoid in the market.

The list is particularly timely as digital asset adoption continues to rise and new regulatory frameworks begin reshaping the crypto landscape in the U.S. and abroad.

Each company featured in the list includes a detailed review, user ratings, security analysis, and fee breakdown-making it easy for users to compare options side by side.

The full Best Crypto Companies in the US List is available to read for free on IRAEmpire's official website.

About IRAEmpire

IRAEmpire.com is a trusted source for financial education, retirement planning insights, and business reviews. The platform empowers individuals to make smarter investment choices with unbiased, in-depth analysis across traditional and alternative asset classes.

Media Contact:

Ryan Paulson

Chief Editor

ryan@iraempire.com

www.iraempire.com

To view the source version of this press release, please visit https://www.newsfilecorp.com/release/261458
